text.title: chaos-eval-fault-injection Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for chaos-eval-fault-injection as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Injecting only "tool_error" because it is the easiest to simulate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The most damaging real-world failure is tool_mutate (corrupted data) — agents react to wrong-but-plausible returns with confident wrong answers. Skipping mutate guarantees you never measure that risk.

failure-mode.description: Grading chaos-eval with the same boolean "success" used for sunny-day eval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sunny-day eval has no faults, so "fail" only means "incorrect". Under fault injection "fail" lumps together honest escalation, retry timeout, and confident wrongness — three completely different reliability stories.
